findings: No lock-bound real C84S command or orchestration entrypoint invokes Stage A, Stage B, the selection freeze, Stage C, and final publication in the required one-way order.

The lock contains per-context artifact loading and selector formulas but no bound production adapter that enumerates all 944 contexts and materializes the complete zero-label, fixed-default, Q0, and access-ledger selection payloads.

The lock contains held-evaluation metric functions and a Stage-C table writer but no bound production adapter that consumes the immutable selection freeze plus evaluation labels and materializes the canonical 18608 method-context rows.

The executable contract does not define how 2048 Q0 chains are integrated into one method-context decision row, including standardized regret, top-k, selected regime, coverage, and applicable measurement outputs.

The S0-S20 synthetic benchmark directly fabricates method-context rows and enters c84s_analysis.run_analysis_and_freeze; it does not execute the real Stage-B-to-Stage-C transformation that is missing.
Creating an ad hoc Python or shell orchestrator after the execution lock would introduce unbound scientific implementation and is therefore forbidden.

required_additive_repair_before_real_lab: Register executable Q0 chain-integration and method-context materialization semantics before implementation.
Implement separate lock-bound Stage-A, Stage-B, and Stage-C real entrypoints with immutable persisted handoffs and an attempt/authorization-consumption ledger.
Implement complete 944-context selection orchestration and exact 18608-row held-evaluation materialization without changing methods, formulas, thresholds, budgets, or taxonomy.

Run a production-path synthetic benchmark through label provisioning, context loading, selection, selection freeze, held evaluation, inference, and atomic final publication without directly injecting method-context rows.
Create a replacement analysis execution lock and obtain a fresh direct PI authorization; this authorization must not migrate.
